citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
There were two instances of significant changes to ongoing protocols without prior review and approval by the IACUC.
- On 1/14/2025, one pig that underwent a minor surgical procedure did not receive the pre-operative buprenorphine
injection that was a part of the approved protocol. All other peri-operative analgesics were administered appropriately. No
animal welfare concerns were identified.
- On 3/26/2025, four rabbits that underwent minor surgical procedures did not receive their second dose of Meloxicam for
post-operative pain management. The first and last doses were administered appropriately. The rabbits were evaluated
by a veterinarian upon identification of the incident. No animal welfare concerns were found.
Performing activities not covered by IACUC-approved protocols can result in harm or death to the impacted animals.
With respect to activities involving animals, the IACUC, as an agent of the research facility, shall review and approve,
require modifications in (to secure approval), or withhold approval of proposed significant changes regarding the care and
use of animals in ongoing activities.
These incidents were identified by the facility prior to inspection and corrective measures were implemented.

citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care.
citations.narrative:
Daily observations of twelve ferrets did not occur for two days over the weekend of 9/20/2025. The ferrets had access to
water, but no food was seen in the enclosure at the next check. The ferrets were evaluated by a veterinarian once the
oversight was identified. No health concerns were found.
Not conducting daily observations of the animals can have negative effects on the health and wellbeing of the animals.
Each research facility shall establish and maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that include daily observation of
all animals to assess their health and well-being; provided, however, that daily observation of animals may be
accomplished by someone other than the attending veterinarian; and provided further, that a mechanism of direct and
frequent communication is required so that timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and
well-being is conveyed to the Attending Veterinarian.
This incident was identified by the facility prior to inspection and corrective measures were implemented. To remain
corrected today, May 14,2026 and forward.

A rabbit kit was found dead in the cage door of its enclosure on 5/6/2025. A necropsy was performed and it was
determined that the kit died from trauma from the door.
Failure to handle animals in a careful manner can negatively impact their health and well-being by resulting in injury,
death, etc.
Handling of all animals shall be done as expeditiously and carefully as possible in a manner that does not cause trauma,
overheating, excessive cooling, behavioral stress, physical harm, or unnecessary discomfort.
This incident was identified by the facility prior to inspection and corrective measures were implemented. To remain
corrected today, May 14,2026 and forward.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.31(c)(7)  Repeat
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
There were two instances of significant changes to ongoing protocols without prior review and approval by the IACUC.  
 
- On 1/14/2025, one pig that underwent a minor surgical procedure did not receive the pre-operative buprenorphine 
injection that was a part of the approved protocol. All other peri-operative analgesics were administered appropriately. No 
animal welfare concerns were identified. 
- On 3/26/2025, four rabbits that underwent minor surgical procedures did not receive their second dose of Meloxicam for 
post-operative pain management. The first and last doses were administered appropriately. The rabbits were evaluated 
by a veterinarian upon identification of the incident. No animal welfare concerns were found. 
 
Performing activities not covered by IACUC-approved protocols can result in harm or death to the impacted animals. 
 
With respect to activities involving animals, the IACUC, as an agent of the research facility, shall review and approve, 
require modifications in (to secure approval), or withhold approval of proposed significant changes regarding the care and 
use of animals in ongoing activities. 
 
These incidents were identified by the facility prior to inspection and corrective measures were implemented. 
2.33(b)(3) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care. 
Daily observations of twelve ferrets did not occur for two days over the weekend of 9/20/2025. The ferrets had access to 
water, but no food was seen in the enclosure at the next check. The ferrets were evaluated by a veterinarian once the 
oversight was identified. No health concerns were found.  
 
Not conducting daily observations of the animals can have negative effects on the health and wellbeing of the animals. 
 
Each research facility shall establish and maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that include daily observation of 
all animals to assess their health and well-being; provided, however, that daily observation of animals may be

accomplished by someone other than the attending veterinarian; and provided further, that a mechanism of direct and 
frequent communication is required so that timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and 
well-being is conveyed to the Attending Veterinarian. 
 
This incident was identified by the facility prior to inspection and corrective measures were implemented. To remain 
corrected today, May 14,2026 and forward. 
2.38(f)(1)  Critical
   
Miscellaneous. 
A rabbit kit was found dead in the cage door of its enclosure on 5/6/2025. A necropsy was performed and it was 
determined that the kit died from trauma from the door.  
 
Failure to handle animals in a careful manner can negatively impact their health and well-being by resulting in injury, 
death, etc. 
 
Handling of all animals shall be done as expeditiously and carefully as possible in a manner that does not cause trauma, 
overheating, excessive cooling, behavioral stress, physical harm, or unnecessary discomfort. 
 
This incident was identified by the facility prior to inspection and corrective measures were implemented. To remain 
corrected today, May 14,2026 and forward. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
